summary: Added a Path Map Proof Cache card that surfaces recent artifactPreview-enabled trail events and lets operators jump the Event Proof lens directly to a proof artifact.

queries: document preview in activity feed dashboard UX pattern
artifact preview detail panel dashboard UX
inline document preview master detail dashboard UI
pattern_notes: A proof cache gives operators a scan-first summary before opening deeper detail.
Preview cards should be compact and clickable into the existing detail surface.
Master-detail systems work better when summary cards and detail lenses share the same selected-record state.

ui_contract: Render up to three recent proof artifacts from artifactPreview-enabled lane trail events.
Use the existing data-path-event-focus path so Proof Cache cards update Node Intel's Event Proof lens.
Show proof artifact labels and first preview lines without adding a new schema or browser file reads.
Keep the cache responsive inside the Path Map bottom grid.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, .path-proof-cache was absent, .path-proof-item count was 0, and Proof Cache text was absent while Event Proof previews existed.

green_route.evidence: The Path Map rendered a Proof Cache with 3 artifact cards. Clicking the first card updated the Event Proof lens to Digital products local gated hold register with 4 preview lines and no browser errors.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered 3 proof cards, updated the Event Proof lens after a proof click, kept document width equal to viewport width, and reported no bad rectangles, console errors, or failed requests.
